parameters.content:
# 🚀 SISTEMA DE VENTAS ENTERPRISE

## 🎯 QUÉ ES ESTE PROYECTO:

**Sistema de ventas completo y profesional** construido con n8n que incluye:

### 🔐 AUTENTICACIÓN:
- Login seguro con Supabase Auth
- JWT tokens reales
- Rate limiting con Redis
- Validación robusta de entrada

### 💰 GESTIÓN DE VENTAS:
- Crear ventas con transacciones atómicas
- Verificar inventario en tiempo real
- Calcular impuestos automáticamente
- Generar números de factura únicos

### 📊 REPORTES Y ANALYTICS:
- Reportes de ventas con filtros
- Exportación a Excel/PDF real
- Paginación segura
- Métricas en tiempo real

### 💾 BACKUP Y MONITOREO:
- Backups automáticos diarios
- Health checks del sistema
- Logs estructurados
- Alertas automáticas

### 🏢 CARACTERÍSTICAS ENTERPRISE:
- Multi-tenant (múltiples empresas)
- Escalable para 10-1000+ usuarios
- Seguridad enterprise
- Performance optimizada

# 📖 CÓMO USAR ESTE SISTEMA

## 🔧 CONFIGURACIÓN INICIAL:

### 1️⃣ CONFIGURAR SUPABASE:
- Crear cuenta en supabase.com (gratis)
- Crear proyecto y obtener URL + API Key
- Configurar usuarios con roles

### 2️⃣ CONFIGURAR BASE DE DATOS:
- Instalar PostgreSQL
- Ejecutar esquema de BD
- Crear usuario de aplicación

### 3️⃣ CONFIGURAR REDIS:
- Instalar Redis
- Configurar password
- Verificar conexión

### 4️⃣ CONFIGURAR APIS EXTERNAS:
- CloudConvert (reportes Excel/PDF)
- WhatsApp Business (opcional)
- Stripe/Mercado Pago (opcional)

## 🚀 USO DEL SISTEMA:

### 🔐 LOGIN:
```bash
POST /webhook/login-real-webhook
{
  "email": "admin@empresa.com",
  "password": "password123"
}
```

### 💰 CREAR VENTA:
```bash
POST /webhook/create-sale-real-webhook
Authorization: Bearer JWT_TOKEN
{
  "customer_info": {
    "name": "Cliente",
    "email": "cliente@email.com"
  },
  "items": [{
    "product_id": 1,
    "quantity": 2,
    "unit_price": 100
  }]
}
```

### 📊 GENERAR REPORTE:
```bash
GET /webhook/report-real-webhook
Authorization: Bearer JWT_TOKEN
?start_date=2024-01-01&end_date=2024-12-31
```

### 🏥 HEALTH CHECK:
```bash
GET /webhook/health-real-webhook
```

## 📋 ENDPOINTS DISPONIBLES:
- `/webhook/login-real-webhook` - Autenticación
- `/webhook/create-sale-real-webhook` - Crear ventas
- `/webhook/report-real-webhook` - Reportes
- `/webhook/health-real-webhook` - Monitoreo

parameters.query: INSERT INTO error_logs (error_type, error_message, context, user_id, company_id, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, NOW())
SELECT p.id, p.name, p.price, p.sku, i.quantity, i.reserved FROM products p LEFT JOIN inventory i ON p.id = i.product_id WHERE p.id = ANY($1) AND p.is_active = true AND p.company_id = $2

WITH new_sale AS (
  INSERT INTO sales (
    company_id, user_id, customer_info, invoice_number,
    total_amount, tax_amount, final_total, payment_method, metadata, created_at
  )
  VALUES ($1, $2, $3::jsonb, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8, $9::jsonb, NOW())
  RETURNING id
),
items_data AS (
  SELECT 
    new_sale.id as sale_id,
    unnest($10::int[]) as product_id,
    unnest($11::int[]) as quantity,
    unnest($12::decimal[]) as unit_price,
    unnest($13::decimal[]) as total_price
  FROM new_sale
)
INSERT INTO sale_items (sale_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price, total_price)
SELECT sale_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price, total_price
FROM items_data;

-- Actualizar inventario
UPDATE inventory
SET 
  quantity = inventory.quantity - updates.qty,
  reserved = inventory.reserved + updates.qty,
  updated_at = NOW()
FROM (
  SELECT 
    unnest($10::int[]) as pid,
    unnest($11::int[]) as qty
) as updates
WHERE inventory.product_id = updates.pid;

-- Retornar ID de venta
SELECT id FROM new_sale;

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { success: true, invoiceNumber: $json.invoice_number, totalAmount: $json.final_total, message: 'Venta registrada exitosamente' } }}

parameters.query: SELECT s.id, s.invoice_number, s.total_amount, s.final_total, s.payment_method, s.created_at, u.profile->>'name' as seller_name, c.name as customer_name FROM sales s LEFT JOIN users u ON s.user_id = u.id LEFT JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.id WHERE s.company_id = $1 AND s.created_at >= $2 AND s.created_at <= $3 ORDER BY s.created_at DESC LIMIT $4 OFFSET $5

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { success: true, data: $('📊 Obtener Reporte Seguro').all().map(item => item.json), exportJob: $json, message: 'Reporte generado exitosamente' } }}

parameters.arguments: -c "pg_dump -h ${DB_HOST:-localhost} -U ${DB_USER:-postgres} -d ${DB_NAME:-ventas} -F c -f /tmp/backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).dump && echo 'Backup completado exitosamente' || echo 'Error en backup'"

parameters.query: INSERT INTO backup_logs (filename, file_path, file_size, file_hash, backup_type, status, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, NOW())
